title: mcp debugging verb — time-travel run interrogation
manager: The debugging half of the `mcp` verb: time-travel tools — state_at, why_rejected, val_history, diverges_from — that let an agent interrogate a recorded run at any point. This is the payoff of deterministic replay: because every run is reproducible, you can step backward, ask why a transition was rejected, or find exactly where two runs diverged — impossible with a live-only debugger. Split from the authoring MCP (Wmcp.1), which ships years earlier; this half waits for the v9-v12 runtime + proof stack it interrogates.
cs: Time-travel tools (state_at / why_rejected / val_history / diverges_from) over recorded runs; the replay-powered debugger, layered onto the fsl-mcp satellite once run-records + proofs exist.
